title: Not made like they used to make them
text:
I always buy Skullcandy but this pair I might as well have bought at the dollar store. Cheap plastic, no bass, crap. I had to buy another because my last Skullcandy pair got a bad wire just from overuse. This is either a rip off brand or Skullcandy got cheap on us. Also none of the ear pieces fit into my ear correctly and it hurts my outer ear canal.

Very disappointed.

title: Bluetooth Earbuds
text:
Update: Battery died after 6 months

I have used Skullcandy before and wasn't much impressed. However I am surprised by these! They have great bass sound. The buttons are easy. There are three size of eartips, the smallest are not great. In fact the middle ones gave the best sound. You can remove the wing/flap part that sticks in your ear.

title: Don't waste your money
text: I have a pair of BeatsX that I wear to work everyday, and wanted a set for home as well. I thought the Sennheiser would be an upgrade, and while they are nice and sound great, they are truly awful earbuds. The huge thing that goes around your neck is ridiculous, and most annoying of all I wanted them for use on my computer and phone as I switch between the two throughout the evening, but they can't be used on one without completely turning off bluetooth on the other device. If you don't turn off the bluetooth on the other device they shout "Disconnected" in your ears every 15 seconds. Just buy the BeatsX, I have used those everyday for about a year now and they sound great, get 8 hours of use, and charge very fast. The new version of these only get 6 hours of battery life so I won't bother with those. Wish I had returned these and gotten a second pair of Beats. I'm back to using my wired earbuds when at home because I can't stand using these things.

title: Don't waste your money
text: I have a pair of BeatsX that I wear to work everyday, and wanted a set for home as well. I thought the Sennheiser would be an upgrade, and while they are nice and sound great, they are truly awful earbuds. The huge thing that goes around your neck is ridiculous, and most annoying of all I wanted them for use on my computer and phone as I switch between the two throughout the evening, but they can't be used on one without completely turning off bluetooth on the other device. If you don't turn off the bluetooth on the other device they shout "Disconnected" in your ears every 15 seconds. Just buy the BeatsX, I have used those everyday for about a year now and they sound great, get 8 hours of use, and charge very fast. The new version of these only get 6 hours of battery life so I won't bother with those. Wish I had returned these and gotten a second pair of Beats. I'm back to using my wired earbuds when at home because I can't stand using these things.

title: Not good.
text: My wife asked me to order these during a Prime Day sale.  They were only $80, and in my opinion, that's still way too much.  There's too much bass, the mids are muddy and the highs are very weak.  Definitely not for listening to rock or metal.  I tried gaming with them, and the experience was just as disappointing (I'd rather plug in with some cheaper Rockville Pro M50's over these.)

title: Can't pick up station I want
text: I'm very disappointed as this seems like a decent radio. The sound was good even on the AM stations. However, it's not sensitive enough to pick up the station I want.  I bought it so that I could listen to the radio without using my computer or phone.  The station I wanted was 94.5 in Dallas.  It will only play 94.9. When I try to tune it back it only picks up static before it goes to a spanish speaking station. I will be requesting a refund.  Too bad.  If sony has one with a digital tuner I will try that.

title: I really want to like these...
text:
This is my first purchase of earbuds in the over $200 category. I got the Jabra Elite 65t as soon as they came out and have since bought a couple of other pairs of earbuds in the under $100 category that had better features like IPX7 or 8hr battery life. Therefore I was excited to upgrade to the XM4 for their bettery life and the noise canceling.

Unfortunately, I've had several issues with these. I don't have particularly small ears. Usually the medium tips fit without issue. On the XM4, I started with the medium tips and tested their air tightness using the app. The small tips don't have any kind of seal on my ears without really shoving them super far down my ear canal, so I decided to stick with the medium size. Unfortunately, I have experienced significant ear fatigue enough so that I can't wear the earbuds for more than a couple of hours. I have since switched to the small tips for the sake of comfort, but that means sacrificing any passive noise canceling - and unless I have a defective unit, without the passive noise canceling in addition to the active noise canceling, there is absolutely no difference between having the noise canceling on and off. To me, this completely defeats the purpose of buying noise canceling earbuds.

I also have had issues with connectivity such as the bluetooth connection on my phone suddenly dropping and having to go into the app to manually pair them again.

In terms of battery life, depending on the control settings of each earbud, there can be a difference in the drain of battery between the left and right side. On a bad day, I had a 30% difference with the right draining significantly faster. My earbuds are using the factory settings with the playback controls on the right and the noise canceling on the left for reference.

Both the connectivity problems and the bettery difference between the earbuds has seemed to have gotten better since performing a factory reset, but be aware that there is still a little difference in the rate of battery drain between the L and R earbuds (It's not super significant now and maybe reaches under 7-8% difference at the end of the day).

I bought some Comply foam tips, which I hope will fix the discomfort issue and make the noise canceling work at the optimal rate without the ear fatigue. However, I shouldn't have to modify almost $300 earbuds to make them work like they should. It would help if Sony would offer more sizes of ear tips to help make them more usable for a wider population. Then again, if I have gripes like these, then maybe the XM4s aren't for me. Makes me super sad considering I think that they're probably fantastic for most people.

title: I really want to like these...
text:
This is my first purchase of earbuds in the over $200 category. I got the Jabra Elite 65t as soon as they came out and have since bought a couple of other pairs of earbuds in the under $100 category that had better features like IPX7 or 8hr battery life. Therefore I was excited to upgrade to the XM4 for their bettery life and the noise canceling.

Unfortunately, I've had several issues with these. I don't have particularly small ears. Usually the medium tips fit without issue. On the XM4, I started with the medium tips and tested their air tightness using the app. The small tips don't have any kind of seal on my ears without really shoving them super far down my ear canal, so I decided to stick with the medium size. Unfortunately, I have experienced significant ear fatigue enough so that I can't wear the earbuds for more than a couple of hours. I have since switched to the small tips for the sake of comfort, but that means sacrificing any passive noise canceling - and unless I have a defective unit, without the passive noise canceling in addition to the active noise canceling, there is absolutely no difference between having the noise canceling on and off. To me, this completely defeats the purpose of buying noise canceling earbuds.

I also have had issues with connectivity such as the bluetooth connection on my phone suddenly dropping and having to go into the app to manually pair them again.

In terms of battery life, depending on the control settings of each earbud, there can be a difference in the drain of battery between the left and right side. On a bad day, I had a 30% difference with the right draining significantly faster. My earbuds are using the factory settings with the playback controls on the right and the noise canceling on the left for reference.

Both the connectivity problems and the bettery difference between the earbuds has seemed to have gotten better since performing a factory reset, but be aware that there is still a little difference in the rate of battery drain between the L and R earbuds (It's not super significant now and maybe reaches under 7-8% difference at the end of the day).

I bought some Comply foam tips, which I hope will fix the discomfort issue and make the noise canceling work at the optimal rate without the ear fatigue. However, I shouldn't have to modify almost $300 earbuds to make them work like they should. It would help if Sony would offer more sizes of ear tips to help make them more usable for a wider population. Then again, if I have gripes like these, then maybe the XM4s aren't for me. Makes me super sad considering I think that they're probably fantastic for most people.

title: I really want to like these...
text:
This is my first purchase of earbuds in the over $200 category. I got the Jabra Elite 65t as soon as they came out and have since bought a couple of other pairs of earbuds in the under $100 category that had better features like IPX7 or 8hr battery life. Therefore I was excited to upgrade to the XM4 for their bettery life and the noise canceling.

Unfortunately, I've had several issues with these. I don't have particularly small ears. Usually the medium tips fit without issue. On the XM4, I started with the medium tips and tested their air tightness using the app. The small tips don't have any kind of seal on my ears without really shoving them super far down my ear canal, so I decided to stick with the medium size. Unfortunately, I have experienced significant ear fatigue enough so that I can't wear the earbuds for more than a couple of hours. I have since switched to the small tips for the sake of comfort, but that means sacrificing any passive noise canceling - and unless I have a defective unit, without the passive noise canceling in addition to the active noise canceling, there is absolutely no difference between having the noise canceling on and off. To me, this completely defeats the purpose of buying noise canceling earbuds.

I also have had issues with connectivity such as the bluetooth connection on my phone suddenly dropping and having to go into the app to manually pair them again.

In terms of battery life, depending on the control settings of each earbud, there can be a difference in the drain of battery between the left and right side. On a bad day, I had a 30% difference with the right draining significantly faster. My earbuds are using the factory settings with the playback controls on the right and the noise canceling on the left for reference.

Both the connectivity problems and the bettery difference between the earbuds has seemed to have gotten better since performing a factory reset, but be aware that there is still a little difference in the rate of battery drain between the L and R earbuds (It's not super significant now and maybe reaches under 7-8% difference at the end of the day).

I bought some Comply foam tips, which I hope will fix the discomfort issue and make the noise canceling work at the optimal rate without the ear fatigue. However, I shouldn't have to modify almost $300 earbuds to make them work like they should. It would help if Sony would offer more sizes of ear tips to help make them more usable for a wider population. Then again, if I have gripes like these, then maybe the XM4s aren't for me. Makes me super sad considering I think that they're probably fantastic for most people.

I really want to like these... This is my first purchase of earbuds in the over $200 category. I got the Jabra Elite 65t as soon as they came out and have since bought a couple of other pairs of earbuds in the under $100 category that had better features like IPX7 or 8hr battery life. Therefore I was excited to upgrade to the XM4 for their bettery life and the noise canceling.

Unfortunately, I've had several issues with these. I don't have particularly small ears. Usually the medium tips fit without issue. On the XM4, I started with the medium tips and tested their air tightness using the app. The small tips don't have any kind of seal on my ears without really shoving them super far down my ear canal, so I decided to stick with the medium size. Unfortunately, I have experienced significant ear fatigue enough so that I can't wear the earbuds for more than a couple of hours. I have since switched to the small tips for the sake of comfort, but that means sacrificing any passive noise canceling - and unless I have a defective unit, without the passive noise canceling in addition to the active noise canceling, there is absolutely no difference between having the noise canceling on and off. To me, this completely defeats the purpose of buying noise canceling earbuds.

I also have had issues with connectivity such as the bluetooth connection on my phone suddenly dropping and having to go into the app to manually pair them again.

In terms of battery life, depending on the control settings of each earbud, there can be a difference in the drain of battery between the left and right side. On a bad day, I had a 30% difference with the right draining significantly faster. My earbuds are using the factory settings with the playback controls on the right and the noise canceling on the left for reference.

Both the connectivity problems and the bettery difference between the earbuds has seemed to have gotten better since performing a factory reset, but be aware that there is still a little difference in the rate of battery drain between the L and R earbuds (It's not super significant now and maybe reaches under 7-8% difference at the end of the day).

I bought some Comply foam tips, which I hope will fix the discomfort issue and make the noise canceling work at the optimal rate without the ear fatigue. However, I shouldn't have to modify almost $300 earbuds to make them work like they should. It would help if Sony would offer more sizes of ear tips to help make them more usable for a wider population. Then again, if I have gripes like these, then maybe the XM4s aren't for me. Makes me super sad considering I think that they're probably fantastic for most people.

title: Not great quality
text: These JVC Bluetooth earbuds all have the same issues. The sound is pretty good but the controls kind of suck. The + and - button are both for volume and for skipping through tracks. But it is a guess as to whether you are going to get one or the other. Holding down the buttons should skip back or forward a song. Sometimes it does and sometimes it cranks the volume. My last JVC buds died after a few months. These seem to be the exact same so I have little expectations that these will last.

title: Ergonomics sacrificed for sound
text: Sound is great. Ergonomics not so much. Relatively heavy ear pieces expected to stay in place based on some assumed universal ear anatomy. If for instance you try to run with them, prepare to continually fidget with them as you try to keep them jammed in your ears.
review_full: Ergonomics sacrificed for sound Sound is great. Ergonomics not so much. Relatively heavy ear pieces expected to stay in place based on some assumed universal ear anatomy. If for instance you try to run with them, prepare to continually fidget with them as you try to keep them jammed in your ears.

product_title: Audio-Technica ATH-M50xBT2 Wireless Over-Ear Headphones, Black
features: ['Wireless, on-the-go design delivers the same critically acclaimed sonic performance as the original ATH-M50x professional studio headphones. Compatible Bluetooth Profiles-A2DP, AVRCP, HFP, HSP', 'Proprietary 45 mm large-aperture drivers and dedicated amplifier deliver exceptional clarity throughout an extended frequency range, with deep, accurate bass response', 'Low latency mode improves synchronicity between audio and video for smooth streaming and gaming', 'Premium audio-grade components, including an AK4331 advanced audio DAC and dedicated internal headphone amp, deliver pure, natural sound', 'Dual mics and beamforming technology ensure crystal-clear call quality']
description: ['When we brought the legendary ATH-M50x studio sound into the world of personal wireless audio, we created what are, simply, the best-sounding wireless headphones on the market. But we weren’t content to stop there. Introducing the ATH-M50xBT2, the second generation of our critically acclaimed M-Series wireless over-ear headphones, featuring a number of enhancements to improve performance and usability. But don’t worry – we’ve changed nothing about the critically acclaimed sonic signature. The M50xBT2 delivers the same exhilarating wireless listening experience as its predecessor, offering exceptional clarity and deep, accurate bass response. There’s even the option to adjust the headphone’s EQ settings, if you so choose – make the changes in the A-T Connect app and save directly to the headphones. In addition, we have greatly improved vocal pickup, incorporating beamforming microphone technology for clearer phone calls and communication with the built-in Amazon Alexa voice assistant. We’ve also added multipoint pairing to let you connect wirelessly to two devices at once. An updated USB-C connection, a low latency mode, and compatibility with high-quality audio codecs round out the enhancements.']

title: Once again, right earbud nearly dead
text: This is the second pair of identical Bose headphones I bought from Amazon over the past 3 years. This pair that I bought in November of 2020 is having the same issue as the one I bought in 2018. Out of nowhere the right sound earbud goes very tinny and silent. I tried resetting the headphones by plugging in waiting 5 seconds, and then unplugging and waiting a minute. None of these issues have helped. The last time this happened and sent me a refurbished set. I will be contacting customer support again and letting them know how unhappy I am with this product.

title: Deeply discounted for a reason...
text: Like a few others had mentioned in their reviews, the bluetooth on these is barely functional.  I've connected to a few devices and it is definitely the headphones that intermittently cut out rendering these pretty useless.  I've connected with the provided cable and the sound is ok, as is the noise cancellation.  For me, they are fairly comfortable up to about an hour, after that their is just a little too much pressure on my ears causing soreness.  Overall, bluetooth headphones that don't have functioning bluetooth are just overpriced wired headphones.  Be prepared to be underwhelmed.

title: Deeply discounted for a reason...
text: Like a few others had mentioned in their reviews, the bluetooth on these is barely functional.  I've connected to a few devices and it is definitely the headphones that intermittently cut out rendering these pretty useless.  I've connected with the provided cable and the sound is ok, as is the noise cancellation.  For me, they are fairly comfortable up to about an hour, after that their is just a little too much pressure on my ears causing soreness.  Overall, bluetooth headphones that don't have functioning bluetooth are just overpriced wired headphones.  Be prepared to be underwhelmed.

title: Alerts and calls are freaking loud.
text:
You guys with your bass quality are all crazy.

If you use this with any device that is getting incoming alerts (texts, email or alerts with other apps) you have to adjust the volume for every one of those things individually on each device.

Otherwise, after your set a comfortable volume for whatever you are listening to, you will get a 98+ ddB blast of sound.

Sony confirms that there is no way to control volume in general.
review_full:
Alerts and calls are freaking loud. You guys with your bass quality are all crazy.

title: Soundpeats Model Q2 - Stopped Working within 6 weeks of purchase
text: I bought the Soundpeats Q2 to use while I run/jog. They are very lightweight and fit nice and snug. But first problem was the volume up/down stopped working within 3 weeks of purchase (in about using it 9 times). I was still OK with that since I can still use my phone's volume keys - not convenient, but works. However, within 6 weeks of purchase (so about 15 times using it) they completely stopped working. I have tried charging them, but nothing. The red light comes on for few seconds and then nothing - completely dead. What good is the ergonomics if they just won't work at all!! Don't buy these ever. That's 30 bucks I'll likely never see again (unless Soundpeats you are willing to give a refund!)

title: Great Sound Quality, But Bug-Ridden Earbuds That Won't Hold a Charge
text: The sound on these Bluetooth wireless earbuds is GREAT. However, I'm on my FOURTH pair now (after having returned the previous three to the retailer) because they break after only a month or so. They stop holding a charge. I've tried everything to find a hack for this problem and simply cannot. I try not to overcharge them. Yet, if you don't charge them enough, they won't work. I can't work out without music. I thought these wireless earbuds were the answer to my prayers in terms of being able to work out without holding my iPhone at the gym. I've since gone back to old-school earbuds with a wire because these Bluetooth earbuds are simply UNRELIABLE and bug-ridden. Unless someone can give me firm answers as to why this is happening, I stand by this negative review. Sorry Skullcandy. The only way I can get them to work is by plugging them into my laptop. So sad. I really love the sound quality of these Ink'd wireless earbuds.

title: Plastic Broke: Unusable
text:
Do NOT buy these!
I got these as a christmas gift and after a few months, of which I took IMPECCABLE care of these headphones, the plastic part snapped on one side and started to crack on the other. I loved the sound quality and the battery life was decent. Now I can’t even use my headphones and I am so disappointed in the quality of this product. I also read another thread on an online forum of this happening to a lot if people and SkullCandy apparently will not cover it with their limited warranty.

title: Plastic Broke: Unusable
text:
Do NOT buy these!
I got these as a christmas gift and after a few months, of which I took IMPECCABLE care of these headphones, the plastic part snapped on one side and started to crack on the other. I loved the sound quality and the battery life was decent. Now I can’t even use my headphones and I am so disappointed in the quality of this product. I also read another thread on an online forum of this happening to a lot if people and SkullCandy apparently will not cover it with their limited warranty.

title: Is my skull on a diet? No sweetness to this candy!
text: Are these the "diet" version of so-called "candy"?  Horrible sound quality.  Dollar store buds sound better.  No bass at all, very tinny sound no matter what my equalizer settings were.  Annoying outside noises seem to penetrate selectively.  I have misplaced three good quality earbuds so I was really excited about having some I could find with Tile.  Doesn't it figure, that I will be able to find these when I won't even want them back???  The price was almost 60% off, but these have turned me off to the Skull Candy brand completely.  I will keep them as backups but my search for decent earbuds continues.  Onward to better brands who haven't (yet) sold out on their good reputations.  I guess you get what you pay for, but if I had paid the (alleged) retail of $60 I would be returning these immediately.

title: Was obviously an open box or return and didn't work
text:
I've always liked Anker. But this was a disappointing purchase. The box was clearly previously opened. The ear canal part was dirty and looked used. I could not get it to connect or work in any way. I suspect the unit was returned because it didn't work and somehow made it back into the supply chain as new.

I may try another Anker but as long as I was returning these, I will likely not buy the same give this experience.

title: Was obviously an open box or return and didn't work
text:
I've always liked Anker. But this was a disappointing purchase. The box was clearly previously opened. The ear canal part was dirty and looked used. I could not get it to connect or work in any way. I suspect the unit was returned because it didn't work and somehow made it back into the supply chain as new.

I may try another Anker but as long as I was returning these, I will likely not buy the same give this experience.

title: Died within a year
text: After less than a year of use, during a bike ride they started vibrating (as if a call was coming in), and wouldn't turn off.  The on-off switch did nothing.  Kept vibrating for several hours until the battery died, now the headset is dead.  This was too much money to spend for something to die in less than a year of mild use.  Not going to buy LG again.

title: Degrades quickly, irritating, and Bluetooth is garbage
text:
7 Month Update: Knocking off the stars I gave before, because the cable sheath has already fallen apart (Bose, you've been screwing this up for at least eight years of revisions - fix this already!), the microphone doesn't work anymore, and the battery life is now compete garbage (3 hours on a good day).

Additionally, the low battery warning is obnoxious, long, frequent, and starts too early. It mutes what you're listening to, and the voice says "Battery low. Please charge now." I know that "battery low" means it needs charging, and you don't have to talk over my podcast every two minutes to remind me and reinform me that it needs to be charged. I will not charge now - I will use it until either the battery dies or I get to a point where it's convenient to charge. The only thing the stupid voice accomplishes is to irritate me.

After eight years of Bose headphones, it seems likely this will be my last - they clearly have trouble learning from their mistakes, and they've made many with this model. Not even remotely worth the premium price tag.

Original review :
I imagine that my disappointment has a lot to do with Bluetooth or the limitations of being wireless, but coming from the simplicity of wired headphones, these have been driving me up the wall. The five or so hours of battery life I get doesn't come close to what I need from it. The voice fails miserably at pronouncing the names of all of my devices (though I guess we can't blame any technology for the ambiguity that is English pronunciation). The new sound blocking earpieces hurt my ears (coming from the old style, without the cone), though with the old ones on, it doesn't get loud enough (and would probably eat the battery if it did). The wire casing is pretty concerning for longevity, but this has always been a problem with Bose's earbuds; I'll probably wind up reinforcing it with some macrame like I did with my old wired ones.

Maybe it deserves a fourth star, given what it is, but one star off for the troubles wrought by this path in technology we're taking.

title: Not worth it
text: I ordered these because my I enjoy listening to music at work but because of my iPhone 7 I can’t charge my phone and listen to music at the same time. After about 2 months, the left side stopped working. I ended up contacting the company and they very easily sent a new pair. Now, I was out of a headset for about a month but was glad with the process on getting them replaced. I’ve now had the second set for about 3 months and guess what?? THE RIGHT SIDE STOPPED WORKING. So I would so do not buy these. Just go ahead and spend a little more on a better quality wireless headset. Maybe if you don’t plan on using them all the time it’s a good investment, but it’s not worth it when they’ve broken twice in less than 6 months.

Now, I was out of a headset for about a month but was glad with the process on getting them replaced. I’ve now had the second set for about 3 months and guess what?? THE RIGHT SIDE STOPPED WORKING. So I would so do not buy these. Just go ahead and spend a little more on a better quality wireless headset. Maybe if you don’t plan on using them all the time it’s a good investment, but it’s not worth it when they’ve broken twice in less than 6 months.

title: manufacturing defects
text:
I bought two of these headphones. Both had serious manufacturing defects.

On one, the supplied RCA cord was bad (no sound at all). I replaced this with one from my junk box and all was well.

The other had to be returned. (Aside, one reason I buy from Amazon is their excellent return polices). There simply was no sound to the left earpiece.

While a population of two, of course, is insufficient to make a lasting judgement, it does seem the factory fails the soldering/crimping test. :)

The purpose of these was simply to allow my wife to watch/listen to TV in the middle of the night without awaking me. (one for the small bedroom TV and the other for the monster in the living room). Hence the absence of comments on sound quality.. except to set them up (or try to), I have never had them on.

title: LOVE the headphones, HATE the warranty replacement process.
text:
I LOVE these headphones, so convenient, clear sound, convenient for making phone call, it even reads you the texts you get to your phone. My only issue and it is a big one in my mind is their customer service when I had to send them in for warranty replacement (no longer synched to my bluetooth device). The LG website has live chat which I felt was very convenient and the first conversation went smoothly, I was impressed. There where it ended though. I sent them in and got an email with a link so that I could track the process from there. This link NEVER worked and I had started to think that I might have sent them out to the wrong place. After about two weeks of waiting (and daily live chats to find out what was happening), I was told they were going to be sent out the next day, but I would receive an email notification. Next day, no notification, and then two days later there is a note on my house door from Fedex. Fedex had tried to deliver and I wasn't there. Now I have them scheduled to deliver on Saturday, and I hope it all works out.

Also, the headphones don't sync with Laptops (I have a Dell). I have researched the forums and talked to LG support, but it seems this is a known issue. So if you intend to use these exclusively with your lap top, don't bother.

title: Absolutely aggravating
text:
I am soooo disappointed with this headphone. I bought my Sony over a Bose because of the reviews. Right out of the box I had major problems.
First, you cannot charge them and keep listening to your program. Plugging the headphones in shuts off Bluetooth. So midway through a movie or such you hear the battery low warning, and plug the headphone in, and dead it goes. In my case, I have to re-pair to myAppleTV or Samsung TV after each charge. Then, somehow, the controls are just where my fingers / thumb grab the headphone to place them on my head. I manage to hit a button that moves my setting to “ambient” mode or over from that, and I fade in and out of my movie. Clear sound for a few minutes, then a click and nothing. I’d much rather use a cheaper headphone my wife bought through a local store that hooks up to the Apple TV and stays there. So many good things with this headphone become such a headache that I lost my temper and threw them away. Several hundred dollars I know, but the aggravation is just not worth elevating my blood pressure.
Strangely, a cheap pair of in your ear Sony’s that cling around your neck and have wires up to you ears, do not lose connection when you plug them in, reconnect when using the after charging them, and hold connection without switching through several modes. Go figure. Just under $40. A whole lot better.
Finally, had I bought these from Amazon I would have returned them. Amazon is offering an excellent return time and has a great return policy. Instead, the store I bought them from pointed out their 7 day return policy then go to the manufacturer for all else. Sony unapologetically told me everything was working normally. Ergo, the 2 star rating.

title: Absolutely aggravating
text:
I am soooo disappointed with this headphone. I bought my Sony over a Bose because of the reviews. Right out of the box I had major problems.
First, you cannot charge them and keep listening to your program. Plugging the headphones in shuts off Bluetooth. So midway through a movie or such you hear the battery low warning, and plug the headphone in, and dead it goes. In my case, I have to re-pair to myAppleTV or Samsung TV after each charge. Then, somehow, the controls are just where my fingers / thumb grab the headphone to place them on my head. I manage to hit a button that moves my setting to “ambient” mode or over from that, and I fade in and out of my movie. Clear sound for a few minutes, then a click and nothing. I’d much rather use a cheaper headphone my wife bought through a local store that hooks up to the Apple TV and stays there. So many good things with this headphone become such a headache that I lost my temper and threw them away. Several hundred dollars I know, but the aggravation is just not worth elevating my blood pressure.
Strangely, a cheap pair of in your ear Sony’s that cling around your neck and have wires up to you ears, do not lose connection when you plug them in, reconnect when using the after charging them, and hold connection without switching through several modes. Go figure. Just under $40. A whole lot better.
Finally, had I bought these from Amazon I would have returned them. Amazon is offering an excellent return time and has a great return policy. Instead, the store I bought them from pointed out their 7 day return policy then go to the manufacturer for all else. Sony unapologetically told me everything was working normally. Ergo, the 2 star rating.

title: Absolutely aggravating
text:
I am soooo disappointed with this headphone. I bought my Sony over a Bose because of the reviews. Right out of the box I had major problems.
First, you cannot charge them and keep listening to your program. Plugging the headphones in shuts off Bluetooth. So midway through a movie or such you hear the battery low warning, and plug the headphone in, and dead it goes. In my case, I have to re-pair to myAppleTV or Samsung TV after each charge. Then, somehow, the controls are just where my fingers / thumb grab the headphone to place them on my head. I manage to hit a button that moves my setting to “ambient” mode or over from that, and I fade in and out of my movie. Clear sound for a few minutes, then a click and nothing. I’d much rather use a cheaper headphone my wife bought through a local store that hooks up to the Apple TV and stays there. So many good things with this headphone become such a headache that I lost my temper and threw them away. Several hundred dollars I know, but the aggravation is just not worth elevating my blood pressure.
Strangely, a cheap pair of in your ear Sony’s that cling around your neck and have wires up to you ears, do not lose connection when you plug them in, reconnect when using the after charging them, and hold connection without switching through several modes. Go figure. Just under $40. A whole lot better.
Finally, had I bought these from Amazon I would have returned them. Amazon is offering an excellent return time and has a great return policy. Instead, the store I bought them from pointed out their 7 day return policy then go to the manufacturer for all else. Sony unapologetically told me everything was working normally. Ergo, the 2 star rating.

title: Absolutely aggravating
text:
I am soooo disappointed with this headphone. I bought my Sony over a Bose because of the reviews. Right out of the box I had major problems.
First, you cannot charge them and keep listening to your program. Plugging the headphones in shuts off Bluetooth. So midway through a movie or such you hear the battery low warning, and plug the headphone in, and dead it goes. In my case, I have to re-pair to myAppleTV or Samsung TV after each charge. Then, somehow, the controls are just where my fingers / thumb grab the headphone to place them on my head. I manage to hit a button that moves my setting to “ambient” mode or over from that, and I fade in and out of my movie. Clear sound for a few minutes, then a click and nothing. I’d much rather use a cheaper headphone my wife bought through a local store that hooks up to the Apple TV and stays there. So many good things with this headphone become such a headache that I lost my temper and threw them away. Several hundred dollars I know, but the aggravation is just not worth elevating my blood pressure.
Strangely, a cheap pair of in your ear Sony’s that cling around your neck and have wires up to you ears, do not lose connection when you plug them in, reconnect when using the after charging them, and hold connection without switching through several modes. Go figure. Just under $40. A whole lot better.
Finally, had I bought these from Amazon I would have returned them. Amazon is offering an excellent return time and has a great return policy. Instead, the store I bought them from pointed out their 7 day return policy then go to the manufacturer for all else. Sony unapologetically told me everything was working normally. Ergo, the 2 star rating.

title: Speakers work, but microphone dead on arrival
text:
I noticed that a bunch of people recently had issues with their Sennheiser's quality, but I hoped I those were the exceptions. I guess not. My headset arrived factory sealed (and on time), but after close to an hour of fiddling with it I determined that yep, the mic was broken.

You can hear a buzz when it's on, and that buzz disappears when you click the mute switch. And sometimes you can barely make out your voice coming through. Barely. I tried my wife's El Cheapo headset and that worked fine in the same jacks, so yes, the Sennheisers were bad.

Judging from the timeline of good vs. bad reviews, it seems the bad reviews are also the more recent ones. Whatever the reason, I'd shy away from these.

title: Too bad they discontinued the "old" design
text: New design is a downgrade.  I have three of the old models which work great.  After seven years I started getting a loose connection in the volume control on one of them so I ordered a new pair which came with a new design.  The "double" strap over the head is awkward and does not work well.  They also changed the IR signal function so it does not work as well as the old units.  The reason I know this is because on the old ones there was enough of a range that you could still hear up to 10 feet to the left or right of the transmitter.  In fact I had a large mirror on a side wall and the IR signal would bounce off of that so I could still hear in an adjacent room behind a wall.  With this new design, you must be directly in front of the transmitter or you will get no sound.  I wish I could buy another of the old model but alas they have been discontinued.  Now I need to start searching for something else to replace these as they wear out.

title: Too bad they discontinued the "old" design
text: New design is a downgrade.  I have three of the old models which work great.  After seven years I started getting a loose connection in the volume control on one of them so I ordered a new pair which came with a new design.  The "double" strap over the head is awkward and does not work well.  They also changed the IR signal function so it does not work as well as the old units.  The reason I know this is because on the old ones there was enough of a range that you could still hear up to 10 feet to the left or right of the transmitter.  In fact I had a large mirror on a side wall and the IR signal would bounce off of that so I could still hear in an adjacent room behind a wall.  With this new design, you must be directly in front of the transmitter or you will get no sound.  I wish I could buy another of the old model but alas they have been discontinued.  Now I need to start searching for something else to replace these as they wear out.

title: There is a reason these headphones are on sale.  Sound was ok, but not what was expected
text:
I was looking for a robust sound and high quality headphone. These are a great idea, but they need more work. I was very disappointed. The sound quality was ok, but when turned all the way up, the volume was lower than the cheap earbuds I use at the gym.
The controls are sketchy and I had a difficult time getting everything to work as advertised.

title: There is a reason these headphones are on sale.  Sound was ok, but not what was expected
text:
I was looking for a robust sound and high quality headphone. These are a great idea, but they need more work. I was very disappointed. The sound quality was ok, but when turned all the way up, the volume was lower than the cheap earbuds I use at the gym.
The controls are sketchy and I had a difficult time getting everything to work as advertised.

title: They Don't Last
text:
I love how these fit. They're very light and you don't really notice that they are there. They were easily paired with my phone. I use them at the gym. They have good range, I was able to put my phone down and utilize several stations without any interference or interruption to the device. The verbal prompts that indicate power on/off and connected are a nice feature, I like knowing that it's working and confirmation that it is off. The sound quality was acceptable to me, but I'm not an audiophile, so as long as I can hear my music, I'm good.

Edit:
I wanted to love these, and originally I did. However, I've now gone through two pairs with the second one not lasting nearly as long as the first. I use these only in the gym and liked their fit and range but I don't think they handle the sweat well. Both sets showed severe corrosion when I peeked inside. If you don't plan on sweating when you use these they may be a good buy, but if you do, I'd go with something else.

title: Stopped working - never would connect to my new Sony TV
text:
I got these headsets during the COVID pandemic to help me work from home when others were being noisy in the house. I used them maybe 20 times before they stopped working consistently. It seems like I have a bum battery because after about 10-20 minutes they would disconnect from my computer or cell phone and/or the noise-canceling feature would stop working. I can normally get the headsets working for 15-20 minutes when I need them again but given that the headsets work such a short period of time, it is not worth it.

Given the cost, I am extremely disappointed. I also could never get the headphones to work on my new Sony TV. The TV would not see the Sony blue tooth connection...I did love the fact that my headsets connect to two devices though and loved the color of the headset itself.

title: Stopped working - never would connect to my new Sony TV
text:
I got these headsets during the COVID pandemic to help me work from home when others were being noisy in the house. I used them maybe 20 times before they stopped working consistently. It seems like I have a bum battery because after about 10-20 minutes they would disconnect from my computer or cell phone and/or the noise-canceling feature would stop working. I can normally get the headsets working for 15-20 minutes when I need them again but given that the headsets work such a short period of time, it is not worth it.

Given the cost, I am extremely disappointed. I also could never get the headphones to work on my new Sony TV. The TV would not see the Sony blue tooth connection...I did love the fact that my headsets connect to two devices though and loved the color of the headset itself.

title: Unusable. Excellent marketing, poor engineering.
text:
I will return these shortly.

I can hear calls but they report me cutting in and out. Carefully tested under controlled circumstances.

Touch controls are straight up disastrous. I never know what is going to happen when I try to control them.

App solution on android... well, just go read the reports there. Users are very unhappy.

The things that work well on the Sony Wh-1000xm4 also work just as well on less expensive headphones.

title: Unusable. Excellent marketing, poor engineering.
text:
I will return these shortly.

I can hear calls but they report me cutting in and out. Carefully tested under controlled circumstances.

Touch controls are straight up disastrous. I never know what is going to happen when I try to control them.

App solution on android... well, just go read the reports there. Users are very unhappy.

The things that work well on the Sony Wh-1000xm4 also work just as well on less expensive headphones.

Unusable. Excellent marketing, poor engineering. I will return these shortly.

I can hear calls but they report me cutting in and out. Carefully tested under controlled circumstances.

Touch controls are straight up disastrous. I never know what is going to happen when I try to control them.

The things that work well on the Sony Wh-1000xm4 also work just as well on less expensive headphones.

title: Good sound, but quickly breaks
text: I like the sound quality, but they tend to break very reliably after 2 - 3 months.  It's always that one of the ear-buds stops producing sound.  I'm not hard on the ear-buds--I only use them for exercising once a day--but, regardless, they don't last more than 4 months tops.

I'm not hard on the ear-buds--I only use them for exercising once a day--but, regardless, they don't last more than 4 months tops.

title: OK, but  has some serious usability issues
text:
Sound quality: Good - But, use with my Yamaha electronic drums is impossible, as the headphones contain circuitry, which constantly clamps the volume.
Volume controls: VERY poor. The volume control is very small and I often confuse it with the tuning knob.
Comfort: Ok. They are a bit heavy and lack good head adjustments.
Battery charger-base: VERY poor. Setting the headphones onto the base, battery charger is somewhat difficult and takes some fiddeling with, every time!

title: They are noise canceling but lack sound
text: These headphones are pretty quiet in all the wrong ways.  I can't hear anything around me,  so they are noise canceling.  They fit amazing,  best fit of any headphone ever and are really comfortable.  It's too bad that they are so quiet. I have really cheap headphones which provide more sound.  I'm on the fence about returning them because they are so comfortable.

text: These headphones are pretty quiet in all the wrong ways.  I can't hear anything around me,  so they are noise canceling.  They fit amazing,  best fit of any headphone ever and are really comfortable.  It's too bad that they are so quiet. I have really cheap headphones which provide more sound.  I'm on the fence about returning them because they are so comfortable.

title: Still hear the Demons
text:
This review is for people that care about secure fit/comfort, sound quality (loudness), battery-charging/durability and quality/convenience (protrusion, physical stability, size). If you are concerned of whether or not these are sweat-proof, "look cool", have "sleek packaging" or "easy to pair", then go read the 5000 other reviews that care about these types of non-critical-never-a-problem-time wasting minutia.

I need earbuds that will stay in your ear during physical activity (running) and will fit inside my motorcycle helmet that will cancel out background sound and are loud enough to block out the goblins in my mind. I have yet to find a pair of wireless earbuds that meet my requirements.

Secure Fit/Comfort- Terrible: These things are bulky and heavy. Although, the ear-fins keep them "steady" none of the rubber ear pieces (includes 3) make a tight fit. This ruins the sound quality (allows too much ambient sound) and undermines the entire product. Their weight also puts odd pressure on your inner ear which results in discomfort/pain. I bought a motorcycle helmet that is Xlarge so I would have room to stuff earbuds in my ears and then cram my melon into the helmet. If you jack your head around and pull-push-squeeze-cram you can eventually get your head into the helmet with these buds still-in your ears but, it's all for nothing because as soon as you get moving the wind-ambient sound all-but ruins the volume/quality.

Sound Quality-Average: Since much of the sound quality is dependent on the source; Samsung LGG3-Pandora. If you turn everything up to it's maximum levels and are sitting on your butt on a couch with no other outside noises...they sound average. Considering the cost of these earbuds drops them to below average.

Battery charging, durability, quality -Good: They charge with a standard USB/micro connection (no special adapter needed) and they feel heavy and well built. This is probably the only good aspect of this product.

My current pair are the JayBird BlueBuds X Sport and they are the only pair thus far that meet my particular needs. Unfortunately, the battery is near death (3 years old) and these BlueBuds still do not stay securely in place while exercising and/or stuffed into a helmet. Although, they do a much better job at sealing out ambient noise, I had hoped that after 3 years somebody (Bose, JayBird, Beats ?) would have elevated/advanced the most critical elements of wireless earbuds but, apparently not. We still read lame reviews of earbuds that address shipping/packaging, pairing (even the cheapest Bluetooth electronic pairs easily) and aesthetics (who cares what color, style, shape...just make them loud and stay secure!). I guess I'll go try and find another pair of BlueBuds and maybe crazy glue-silicone the ear-tips permanently in my ear and finally quiet the demons in my head

I need earbuds that will stay in your ear during physical activity (running) and will fit inside my motorcycle helmet that will cancel out background sound and are loud enough to block out the goblins in my mind. I have yet to find a pair of wireless earbuds that meet my requirements.

Secure Fit/Comfort- Terrible: These things are bulky and heavy. Although, the ear-fins keep them "steady" none of the rubber ear pieces (includes 3) make a tight fit. This ruins the sound quality (allows too much ambient sound) and undermines the entire product. Their weight also puts odd pressure on your inner ear which results in discomfort/pain. I bought a motorcycle helmet that is Xlarge so I would have room to stuff earbuds in my ears and then cram my melon into the helmet. If you jack your head around and pull-push-squeeze-cram you can eventually get your head into the helmet with these buds still-in your ears but, it's all for nothing because as soon as you get moving the wind-ambient sound all-but ruins the volume/quality.

Sound Quality-Average: Since much of the sound quality is dependent on the source; Samsung LGG3-Pandora. If you turn everything up to it's maximum levels and are sitting on your butt on a couch with no other outside noises...they sound average. Considering the cost of these earbuds drops them to below average.

Battery charging, durability, quality -Good: They charge with a standard USB/micro connection (no special adapter needed) and they feel heavy and well built. This is probably the only good aspect of this product.

My current pair are the JayBird BlueBuds X Sport and they are the only pair thus far that meet my particular needs. Unfortunately, the battery is near death (3 years old) and these BlueBuds still do not stay securely in place while exercising and/or stuffed into a helmet. Although, they do a much better job at sealing out ambient noise, I had hoped that after 3 years somebody (Bose, JayBird, Beats ?) would have elevated/advanced the most critical elements of wireless earbuds but, apparently not. We still read lame reviews of earbuds that address shipping/packaging, pairing (even the cheapest Bluetooth electronic pairs easily) and aesthetics (who cares what color, style, shape...just make them loud and stay secure!). I guess I'll go try and find another pair of BlueBuds and maybe crazy glue-silicone the ear-tips permanently in my ear and finally quiet the demons in my head

title: Still hear the Demons
text:
This review is for people that care about secure fit/comfort, sound quality (loudness), battery-charging/durability and quality/convenience (protrusion, physical stability, size). If you are concerned of whether or not these are sweat-proof, "look cool", have "sleek packaging" or "easy to pair", then go read the 5000 other reviews that care about these types of non-critical-never-a-problem-time wasting minutia.

I need earbuds that will stay in your ear during physical activity (running) and will fit inside my motorcycle helmet that will cancel out background sound and are loud enough to block out the goblins in my mind. I have yet to find a pair of wireless earbuds that meet my requirements.

Secure Fit/Comfort- Terrible: These things are bulky and heavy. Although, the ear-fins keep them "steady" none of the rubber ear pieces (includes 3) make a tight fit. This ruins the sound quality (allows too much ambient sound) and undermines the entire product. Their weight also puts odd pressure on your inner ear which results in discomfort/pain. I bought a motorcycle helmet that is Xlarge so I would have room to stuff earbuds in my ears and then cram my melon into the helmet. If you jack your head around and pull-push-squeeze-cram you can eventually get your head into the helmet with these buds still-in your ears but, it's all for nothing because as soon as you get moving the wind-ambient sound all-but ruins the volume/quality.

Sound Quality-Average: Since much of the sound quality is dependent on the source; Samsung LGG3-Pandora. If you turn everything up to it's maximum levels and are sitting on your butt on a couch with no other outside noises...they sound average. Considering the cost of these earbuds drops them to below average.

Battery charging, durability, quality -Good: They charge with a standard USB/micro connection (no special adapter needed) and they feel heavy and well built. This is probably the only good aspect of this product.

My current pair are the JayBird BlueBuds X Sport and they are the only pair thus far that meet my particular needs. Unfortunately, the battery is near death (3 years old) and these BlueBuds still do not stay securely in place while exercising and/or stuffed into a helmet. Although, they do a much better job at sealing out ambient noise, I had hoped that after 3 years somebody (Bose, JayBird, Beats ?) would have elevated/advanced the most critical elements of wireless earbuds but, apparently not. We still read lame reviews of earbuds that address shipping/packaging, pairing (even the cheapest Bluetooth electronic pairs easily) and aesthetics (who cares what color, style, shape...just make them loud and stay secure!). I guess I'll go try and find another pair of BlueBuds and maybe crazy glue-silicone the ear-tips permanently in my ear and finally quiet the demons in my head

title: Frustrated
text: I am frustrated with these.  My husband got them for me as a Christmas gift, Christmas day the plastic portion of the power button fell off so we returned that pair (thank you Amazon for great customer servic) and now with my current pair the sound keeps cutting out. It doesn't happen every use but at least every third use and once it starts there is no way to stop it with out turning them off and not using them for a few hours.  I listen to a lot of audio books and the sound is great but the problems I've had are too frustrating to make the sound quality worth keeping them.

text: At first only one speaker worked. Then as soon as I got the 2nd one to work they both quit. When I plug in the 3.5mm plug the water starts bouncong around but no sound. Also I can plug the 3.5mm jack in the speaker and hold the other end in my hand and touch the plug and the water will start bouncing based on my touch. I really wish these worked cause they are really cool and when I did get them to work for a second the sound was decent. title: Battery life sucks!!
text:
In what universe does the battery charge from 20% to 100% in less than 5 seconds ??!!

I bought these slightly over a year ago and I have only used these earphones intermittently; not all day every day. They were performing OK at first, sound quality is not bad at all, connectivity issues are non-existent.

The biggest problem is battery life and the awful Bose customer support. After you charge it it will show the green LED, and it will say “battery 100%”, then within 5-10 mins, without you doing anything, the battery will drop to 50% and then 20%.

Software update seemed to fix it for one day and now it’s even worse! At 20% if I charge it the green led comes on in less than 5 seconds and it says 100% and then the story repeats. The (presumably) faulty software (or maybe faulty battery) is not letting the battery charge!

Customer support on their website is worse than DMW!

$149 earphones are now worthless! Never buying Bose again.

title: Battery life sucks!!
text:
In what universe does the battery charge from 20% to 100% in less than 5 seconds ??!!

I bought these slightly over a year ago and I have only used these earphones intermittently; not all day every day. They were performing OK at first, sound quality is not bad at all, connectivity issues are non-existent.

The biggest problem is battery life and the awful Bose customer support. After you charge it it will show the green LED, and it will say “battery 100%”, then within 5-10 mins, without you doing anything, the battery will drop to 50% and then 20%.

Software update seemed to fix it for one day and now it’s even worse! At 20% if I charge it the green led comes on in less than 5 seconds and it says 100% and then the story repeats. The (presumably) faulty software (or maybe faulty battery) is not letting the battery charge!

Customer support on their website is worse than DMW!

$149 earphones are now worthless! Never buying Bose again.

title: DO NOT BUY - design flaw; they will break
text: Now that they're broken and unusable, it's easy to see the flaw in design. The cheap plastic develops a crease where the earpiece hinges at the band. Once this occurs, any use at all will cause them to crack and become unwearable. Took barely more than a year of light use to do this. I went back to the seller and they had quit selling them because of this problem; it took less than a year for moderate to heavy users which meant alot of warranty replacements.
review_full: DO NOT BUY - design flaw; they will break Now that they're broken and unusable, it's easy to see the flaw in design. title: Stopped working after 2.5 months
text:
These headphones worked great (excellent sound, good fit, etc) until they suddenly stopped work. At first the buttons stopped responding, only working again when I restarted everything. Then the left earbud ceased producing any sound. I bought these to use for basic functions (e.g listening to music and making phone calls). They were not used roughly, and spent most of the time around my neck so the sudden cease in function is very disappointing.
When they worked, they were excellent. But that only lasted a little more than 2 months.

Stopped working after 2.5 months These headphones worked great (excellent sound, good fit, etc) until they suddenly stopped work. At first the buttons stopped responding, only working again when I restarted everything. Then the left earbud ceased producing any sound. I bought these to use for basic functions (e.g listening to music and making phone calls). They were not used roughly, and spent most of the time around my neck so the sudden cease in function is very disappointing.
When they worked, they were excellent. But that only lasted a little more than 2 months.

title: Cheap quality and uncomfortable
text:
Very good sound, better than most of the closed ended headphones I have owned.

However, comfort is terrible. This thing causes pain on my ears after just 1 hour of use. Its tight and no matter how far I bend the headset, it doesn't get any better. The earcuffs are too small in diameter to envelope my entire ears. It causes pressure that is very uncomfortable. They need to be at least another 1-2" bigger.

The other thing I don't like, is the cheap plastic. I mean c'mon, this is disappointing for the price and something you'll see on low end headphones. Also, the foam used is cheap and hard.

After three days of use, I couldn't stand using these.

However, comfort is terrible. This thing causes pain on my ears after just 1 hour of use. Its tight and no matter how far I bend the headset, it doesn't get any better. The earcuffs are too small in diameter to envelope my entire ears. It causes pressure that is very uncomfortable. They need to be at least another 1-2" bigger.

The other thing I don't like, is the cheap plastic. I mean c'mon, this is disappointing for the price and something you'll see on low end headphones. Also, the foam used is cheap and hard.

After three days of use, I couldn't stand using these.

product_title: Audio-Technica ATH-M50xBT2 Wireless Over-Ear Headphones, Black
features: ['Wireless, on-the-go design delivers the same critically acclaimed sonic performance as the original ATH-M50x professional studio headphones. Compatible Bluetooth Profiles-A2DP, AVRCP, HFP, HSP', 'Proprietary 45 mm large-aperture drivers and dedicated amplifier deliver exceptional clarity throughout an extended frequency range, with deep, accurate bass response', 'Low latency mode improves synchronicity between audio and video for smooth streaming and gaming', 'Premium audio-grade components, including an AK4331 advanced audio DAC and dedicated internal headphone amp, deliver pure, natural sound', 'Dual mics and beamforming technology ensure crystal-clear call quality']
description: ['When we brought the legendary ATH-M50x studio sound into the world of personal wireless audio, we created what are, simply, the best-sounding wireless headphones on the market. But we weren’t content to stop there. Introducing the ATH-M50xBT2, the second generation of our critically acclaimed M-Series wireless over-ear headphones, featuring a number of enhancements to improve performance and usability. But don’t worry – we’ve changed nothing about the critically acclaimed sonic signature. The M50xBT2 delivers the same exhilarating wireless listening experience as its predecessor, offering exceptional clarity and deep, accurate bass response. There’s even the option to adjust the headphone’s EQ settings, if you so choose – make the changes in the A-T Connect app and save directly to the headphones. In addition, we have greatly improved vocal pickup, incorporating beamforming microphone technology for clearer phone calls and communication with the built-in Amazon Alexa voice assistant. We’ve also added multipoint pairing to let you connect wirelessly to two devices at once. An updated USB-C connection, a low latency mode, and compatibility with high-quality audio codecs round out the enhancements.']

title: Painful, tinny earbuds
text: Bought these (in black) to replace some earclip-equipped Sony headphones that I loved (dog chewed them up).  If I seat them far enough into my ear canals to get decent bass, they hurt my ear in minutes.  Even resting on my ears rather than inserted in them, the part of the headphone just in back of the silicone pads presses on the outside of the ear in a way that is very painful.  I couldn't keep them on for more than 20 minutes max.  The Sonys were comfy for hours at a time.  During the time I had them on, the sound was tinny, with flaccid, weak bass.  Complete waste of money for me.

Complete waste of money for me.

title: Not worth the money
text: Ultimately a great headphone, I wish I could it more stars. In fact, I have owned two of them now. My first pair lasted a year before the speaker blew out. My second one lasted 4 months before the speaker blew out. Sure I listen to loud music with them, but for money and the "professional studio" it should be able to handle what my Fiio player dishes out. So ultimately, buy something better or pay less and ultimately get same quality as these from something cheaper.  Their really just not worth the extra bucks.

title: NOT an "over-ear" headphone...
text:
"Over-ear" is a very poorly chosen term because something "over" your ear might also be considered to be something "on" your ear and vice versa. The proper term that should have been established for use is "around-ear" or "surround-ear" and, in fact, Sennheiser uses the term "around-ear" on their website to describe their headphones which are otherwise listed with the "over-ear" misnomer.

I wonder if the reason the term "over-ear" became the default term was because those who established it were not native English speakers who didn't realize the nuances. Or, perhaps, it was just some native English speaking idiot in marketing.

Anyway, unlike the unambiguously named "on-ear" headphones, "over-ear" headphones are supposed to surround your ears with the pads resting on your skull and not on your ears. With poorly designed versions of those, like some of those which use memory foam in the ear pads, sweating can be an issue. With properly designed ones, it isn't.

What characteristics are required for a proper "over-ear," better called "around-ear" headphone? These:

1. The holes in the center of the headphone pads should be large enough for your ears to fit inside.
2. The headphone pads should be thick enough to completely raise the hard headphone driver (speaker) face away from your ears OR
3. A combination of adequate headphone pad thickness and a rearward sloping of the driver away from the head to prevent contact with the ears which, as you may have noticed, slope away from your head in the rearward direction.

These headphones as well as a previous pair of a different brand that I bought at a similar price do not comply with any of those characteristics. As a further insult, to make up for their too-thin ear pads, they're too rigid in order to reduce the amount they collapse.

Have headphone manufacturers somehow forgotten those required characteristics over years? No, I suspect they are simply cost cutting to compete with Chinese manufacturers and smaller headphones are more stylish and more easily stored for travel.

They suffer little negative market reaction for doing this because most people apparently don't know what an "over-ear" headphone is supposed to be. They put the headphones on and if they work, sound OK, and aren't immediately uncomfortable, give them positive reviews. Also, some reviewers may have very small ears where the headphones actually do act as a "surround-ear" design although with this model and the previous one I also returned, I don't see that as being possible for anyone over 6 years old.

I wonder if the reason the term "over-ear" became the default term was because those who established it were not native English speakers who didn't realize the nuances. Or, perhaps, it was just some native English speaking idiot in marketing.

Anyway, unlike the unambiguously named "on-ear" headphones, "over-ear" headphones are supposed to surround your ears with the pads resting on your skull and not on your ears. With poorly designed versions of those, like some of those which use memory foam in the ear pads, sweating can be an issue. With properly designed ones, it isn't.

What characteristics are required for a proper "over-ear," better called "around-ear" headphone? These:

1. The holes in the center of the headphone pads should be large enough for your ears to fit inside.
2. The headphone pads should be thick enough to completely raise the hard headphone driver (speaker) face away from your ears OR
3. A combination of adequate headphone pad thickness and a rearward sloping of the driver away from the head to prevent contact with the ears which, as you may have noticed, slope away from your head in the rearward direction.

These headphones as well as a previous pair of a different brand that I bought at a similar price do not comply with any of those characteristics. As a further insult, to make up for their too-thin ear pads, they're too rigid in order to reduce the amount they collapse.

Have headphone manufacturers somehow forgotten those required characteristics over years? No, I suspect they are simply cost cutting to compete with Chinese manufacturers and smaller headphones are more stylish and more easily stored for travel.

They suffer little negative market reaction for doing this because most people apparently don't know what an "over-ear" headphone is supposed to be. They put the headphones on and if they work, sound OK, and aren't immediately uncomfortable, give them positive reviews. Also, some reviewers may have very small ears where the headphones actually do act as a "surround-ear" design although with this model and the previous one I also returned, I don't see that as being possible for anyone over 6 years old.

These headphones as well as a previous pair of a different brand that I bought at a similar price do not comply with any of those characteristics. As a further insult, to make up for their too-thin ear pads, they're too rigid in order to reduce the amount they collapse.

Have headphone manufacturers somehow forgotten those required characteristics over years? No, I suspect they are simply cost cutting to compete with Chinese manufacturers and smaller headphones are more stylish and more easily stored for travel.

They suffer little negative market reaction for doing this because most people apparently don't know what an "over-ear" headphone is supposed to be. They put the headphones on and if they work, sound OK, and aren't immediately uncomfortable, give them positive reviews. Also, some reviewers may have very small ears where the headphones actually do act as a "surround-ear" design although with this model and the previous one I also returned, I don't see that as being possible for anyone over 6 years old.

title: NOT an "over-ear" headphone...
text:
"Over-ear" is a very poorly chosen term because something "over" your ear might also be considered to be something "on" your ear and vice versa. The proper term that should have been established for use is "around-ear" or "surround-ear" and, in fact, Sennheiser uses the term "around-ear" on their website to describe their headphones which are otherwise listed with the "over-ear" misnomer.

I wonder if the reason the term "over-ear" became the default term was because those who established it were not native English speakers who didn't realize the nuances. Or, perhaps, it was just some native English speaking idiot in marketing.

Anyway, unlike the unambiguously named "on-ear" headphones, "over-ear" headphones are supposed to surround your ears with the pads resting on your skull and not on your ears. With poorly designed versions of those, like some of those which use memory foam in the ear pads, sweating can be an issue. With properly designed ones, it isn't.

What characteristics are required for a proper "over-ear," better called "around-ear" headphone? These:

1. The holes in the center of the headphone pads should be large enough for your ears to fit inside.
2. The headphone pads should be thick enough to completely raise the hard headphone driver (speaker) face away from your ears OR
3. A combination of adequate headphone pad thickness and a rearward sloping of the driver away from the head to prevent contact with the ears which, as you may have noticed, slope away from your head in the rearward direction.

These headphones as well as a previous pair of a different brand that I bought at a similar price do not comply with any of those characteristics. As a further insult, to make up for their too-thin ear pads, they're too rigid in order to reduce the amount they collapse.

Have headphone manufacturers somehow forgotten those required characteristics over years? No, I suspect they are simply cost cutting to compete with Chinese manufacturers and smaller headphones are more stylish and more easily stored for travel.

They suffer little negative market reaction for doing this because most people apparently don't know what an "over-ear" headphone is supposed to be. They put the headphones on and if they work, sound OK, and aren't immediately uncomfortable, give them positive reviews. Also, some reviewers may have very small ears where the headphones actually do act as a "surround-ear" design although with this model and the previous one I also returned, I don't see that as being possible for anyone over 6 years old.

title: comfortable but terrible sound quality, get JBL instead
text:
Bought these for daily gym use.

Original impression was pretty good, simple and easy to use, comfortable, synced to phone without problem.

Unfortunately the sound quality was so bad I couldn't even listen to them loud enough at the gym to cover all the background noise. The highs are so muddy/tinny, and blown out I could not turn them up even loud enough to hear music. All of my prior bluetooth headphones were under $40, so I'm used to low dollar headphones, but these were much worse than any other pair I had. Returned them.

Do yourself a favor and spend the extra $15 to get the JBL headphones, SO much better sound quality, your ears will thank you!!

title: comfortable but terrible sound quality, get JBL instead
text:
Bought these for daily gym use.

Original impression was pretty good, simple and easy to use, comfortable, synced to phone without problem.

Unfortunately the sound quality was so bad I couldn't even listen to them loud enough at the gym to cover all the background noise. The highs are so muddy/tinny, and blown out I could not turn them up even loud enough to hear music. All of my prior bluetooth headphones were under $40, so I'm used to low dollar headphones, but these were much worse than any other pair I had. Returned them.

Do yourself a favor and spend the extra $15 to get the JBL headphones, SO much better sound quality, your ears will thank you!!

title: Disappointed!
text:
Ok. What I really love here is external speakers. I mean that they do exist. And that they are water resistant. BUT..
I never knew that while I'm talking only right speaker will be playing. And you can hear nothing when you just open your car window. I do understand why only one side speakers work, but the sound is too low for talking over the phone.
Mic won't work well. I'm not sure if this is a problem just for my headset, or common. Going to use it for a couple of days and if it continues I will have to return it. My friend has the same, and his mic seems to work well.
While working with Siri and Google Assistant (I'm using it with 2 phones which is a great feature) fails to hear my commands.
I really hope it's all a malfunction of the device I've received, but if it's a common thing I will havу to return them. (((

title: $300 for apair of headphones that died 3months
text: I finally convinced myself to purchase some high end headphones. What a huge mistake. First they never speed charged at all. Such a dissaointment day one first time battery was low i did the 15min charge an nothing if i don’t charge a min 1-2hrs it wont play 10 min. I thought i was doing something wrong than about 2 months of use they started to charge randomly meaning i could charge them 3 hrs pick them up an hear battery low please charge than cut off. Some days it worked same port same charge cable.i started smelling electric burn in my office or so i thuaght untill i noticed the plastic around the headphone charge port was melted. Turns out it was the headphones. Other issues i could answer a call or atleast connect but if i wanted to actually speak to the caller i would have to scramble for my phone to switc call to headphone an atleast 50° of calls would hang up before i got there. So everycall was a phone scramble nightmare. Such a dissapointment. Im waiting to hear from sony support ive sent 3 messeges now. They had called me once but the headphones where on an the call dropped before i could switch over. Total dissapointment

title: $300 for apair of headphones that died 3months
text: I finally convinced myself to purchase some high end headphones. What a huge mistake. First they never speed charged at all. Such a dissaointment day one first time battery was low i did the 15min charge an nothing if i don’t charge a min 1-2hrs it wont play 10 min. I thought i was doing something wrong than about 2 months of use they started to charge randomly meaning i could charge them 3 hrs pick them up an hear battery low please charge than cut off. Some days it worked same port same charge cable.i started smelling electric burn in my office or so i thuaght untill i noticed the plastic around the headphone charge port was melted. Turns out it was the headphones. Other issues i could answer a call or atleast connect but if i wanted to actually speak to the caller i would have to scramble for my phone to switc call to headphone an atleast 50° of calls would hang up before i got there. So everycall was a phone scramble nightmare. Such a dissapointment. Im waiting to hear from sony support ive sent 3 messeges now. They had called me once but the headphones where on an the call dropped before i could switch over. Total dissapointment

title: Unhappy
text:
This is the first pair of infra-red wireless head phones I've owned. I had a set of Rocketfish that finally went bad. The downer to those was the discomfort of the head set. The headset for this Sony product is very comfortable.
Static is the main negative issue with this product. I can't touch my lap top computer without static drowning out the TV show. There is other instances of static for no apparent reason.
Sad part is, the sound quality when uninterrupted is awesome. I have to assume that the FM version is the better choice and will be making an effort to return these in exchange for the FM model.
I also think this model is best in a very small area. This is no give to the range and the range off to the side is even less then advertised.

Sad part is, the sound quality when uninterrupted is awesome. I have to assume that the FM version is the better choice and will be making an effort to return these in exchange for the FM model.
I also think this model is best in a very small area. This is no give to the range and the range off to the side is even less then advertised.

text: I really liked the idea of wireless headphones (who doesn't?) as it's one less cable to trip over, or step on and break, and the only set I ever saw was down at my local music store for like $200.
