title: Garbage!
text: Bought these in February 2022, and writing this in August 2022. From the start I had problems with the Bluetooth. It will readily connect to two different devices (my phone and computer) but if connected to two devices at the same time the sound with come and go frequently. Now in August I have a hard time turning these things on and off. The button works when it feels like it. I have owned other brands of headphones, even cheaper than these that worked far better. The sound is ok but not spectacular. Again, had Phones that sounded better for less money. I have bought MPOW brand and beat them up while using them and they have lasted years while these Skullcandy I have treated with kid gloves and they break in months. Garbage! Don't waste your money on these.

title: Garbage!
text: Bought these in February 2022, and writing this in August 2022. From the start I had problems with the Bluetooth. It will readily connect to two different devices (my phone and computer) but if connected to two devices at the same time the sound with come and go frequently. Now in August I have a hard time turning these things on and off. The button works when it feels like it. I have owned other brands of headphones, even cheaper than these that worked far better. The sound is ok but not spectacular. Again, had Phones that sounded better for less money. I have bought MPOW brand and beat them up while using them and they have lasted years while these Skullcandy I have treated with kid gloves and they break in months. Garbage! Don't waste your money on these.

title: Decent audio. Constant pairing problems
text: This device is very disappointing. After using the Soundcore Sprit Pro, which is great, this is a total letdown. The audio quality is decent for the price. More importantly, it is so hard to pair. Every time I try connect my iPad I have to disable Bluetooth on my phone and remove and re-add this device in the iPad. It's a major pain, and I have to recommend against this product for this reason alone. If there is a solution to this, I'd love to hear about it.

title: Decent audio. Constant pairing problems
text: This device is very disappointing. After using the Soundcore Sprit Pro, which is great, this is a total letdown. The audio quality is decent for the price. More importantly, it is so hard to pair. Every time I try connect my iPad I have to disable Bluetooth on my phone and remove and re-add this device in the iPad. It's a major pain, and I have to recommend against this product for this reason alone. If there is a solution to this, I'd love to hear about it.

title: DO NOT BUY!  Horrible distortion
text:
There is a lot to like about these buds. Sadly they have an extremely bad flaw which is not getting enough attention by Sony.

They have horrible "tinny" or "metallic" echo, commonly heard in the higher vocal range. This is most noticeable on calls, and podcasts. It's impossible to ignore.

Amazon will not allow me to post a link, but you can search around and find people complaining, there is a good thread on their forum. Search for "Sony WF-1000XM4 metallic echo".

This happens on ANC and. non-ANC modes. Using "hear through" does make it less noticeable.

By the time I noticed this, the return window was closed (didn't use them much for the first month). I have since returned two units to Sony for repair. The second set was even worse than the first, which leads me to think it might be a manufacture defect, rather than a design issue.

I will update the review if this gets resolved. Meanwhile, stay away from these!

The good:
- comfortable for IMEs
- excellent battery life
- when distortion is not present (see above), reasonable sound quality
- good ANC
- a snowballs chance of replacing the battery in a year so they are not just e-waste
- decent app

The bad:
- distortion (see above)
- capacitive touch controls are a bit annoying; prefer the tactile versions on some other deigns
- app has some gimmicks that are not that useful

title: DO NOT BUY!  Horrible distortion
text:
There is a lot to like about these buds. Sadly they have an extremely bad flaw which is not getting enough attention by Sony.

They have horrible "tinny" or "metallic" echo, commonly heard in the higher vocal range. This is most noticeable on calls, and podcasts. It's impossible to ignore.

Amazon will not allow me to post a link, but you can search around and find people complaining, there is a good thread on their forum. Search for "Sony WF-1000XM4 metallic echo".

This happens on ANC and. non-ANC modes. Using "hear through" does make it less noticeable.

By the time I noticed this, the return window was closed (didn't use them much for the first month). I have since returned two units to Sony for repair. The second set was even worse than the first, which leads me to think it might be a manufacture defect, rather than a design issue.

I will update the review if this gets resolved. Meanwhile, stay away from these!

The good:
- comfortable for IMEs
- excellent battery life
- when distortion is not present (see above), reasonable sound quality
- good ANC
- a snowballs chance of replacing the battery in a year so they are not just e-waste
- decent app

The bad:
- distortion (see above)
- capacitive touch controls are a bit annoying; prefer the tactile versions on some other deigns
- app has some gimmicks that are not that useful

title: DO NOT BUY!  Horrible distortion
text:
There is a lot to like about these buds. Sadly they have an extremely bad flaw which is not getting enough attention by Sony.

They have horrible "tinny" or "metallic" echo, commonly heard in the higher vocal range. This is most noticeable on calls, and podcasts. It's impossible to ignore.

Amazon will not allow me to post a link, but you can search around and find people complaining, there is a good thread on their forum. Search for "Sony WF-1000XM4 metallic echo".

This happens on ANC and. non-ANC modes. Using "hear through" does make it less noticeable.

By the time I noticed this, the return window was closed (didn't use them much for the first month). I have since returned two units to Sony for repair. The second set was even worse than the first, which leads me to think it might be a manufacture defect, rather than a design issue.

I will update the review if this gets resolved. Meanwhile, stay away from these!

The good:
- comfortable for IMEs
- excellent battery life
- when distortion is not present (see above), reasonable sound quality
- good ANC
- a snowballs chance of replacing the battery in a year so they are not just e-waste
- decent app

The bad:
- distortion (see above)
- capacitive touch controls are a bit annoying; prefer the tactile versions on some other deigns
- app has some gimmicks that are not that useful

title: Terrible customer service
text:
I think Bose makes great products, usually. I like their quality. I've had good experiences, until now. The product that arrived did not consistently connect to the Bluetooth in my mobile device and laptop. After a few days of having to re-pair the Soundsport with my devices multiple times, while powering everything off and on again, I called Bose to troubleshoot. They immediately said to ship them the headphones and they'd replace them. (This tells me the problem is not unique, but common.) I was told I'd receive the new ones in 2-3 days as a courtesy (usually 10 days), and that they did not need to receive my return before mailing out the new ones. 10 days later when I did not receive anything from Bose, I called and was told they were not in stock. I'd not been contacted at all. I then asked for a refund, which had to be issued through Amazon, where I bought them.
Now I'll decide if I want to take another chance on these headphones and purchase them again.

product_title: Audio-Technica ATH-M50xBT2 Wireless Over-Ear Headphones, Black
features: ['Wireless, on-the-go design delivers the same critically acclaimed sonic performance as the original ATH-M50x professional studio headphones. Compatible Bluetooth Profiles-A2DP, AVRCP, HFP, HSP', 'Proprietary 45 mm large-aperture drivers and dedicated amplifier deliver exceptional clarity throughout an extended frequency range, with deep, accurate bass response', 'Low latency mode improves synchronicity between audio and video for smooth streaming and gaming', 'Premium audio-grade components, including an AK4331 advanced audio DAC and dedicated internal headphone amp, deliver pure, natural sound', 'Dual mics and beamforming technology ensure crystal-clear call quality']
description: ['When we brought the legendary ATH-M50x studio sound into the world of personal wireless audio, we created what are, simply, the best-sounding wireless headphones on the market. But we weren’t content to stop there. Introducing the ATH-M50xBT2, the second generation of our critically acclaimed M-Series wireless over-ear headphones, featuring a number of enhancements to improve performance and usability. But don’t worry – we’ve changed nothing about the critically acclaimed sonic signature. The M50xBT2 delivers the same exhilarating wireless listening experience as its predecessor, offering exceptional clarity and deep, accurate bass response. There’s even the option to adjust the headphone’s EQ settings, if you so choose – make the changes in the A-T Connect app and save directly to the headphones. In addition, we have greatly improved vocal pickup, incorporating beamforming microphone technology for clearer phone calls and communication with the built-in Amazon Alexa voice assistant. We’ve also added multipoint pairing to let you connect wirelessly to two devices at once. An updated USB-C connection, a low latency mode, and compatibility with high-quality audio codecs round out the enhancements.']

title: Disappointing for the price
text:
If this was a $20 headphone, its sin could be forgiven, but with a price of over $100, one cannot excuse a wireless headphone that keeps on clicking and making reconnecting sounds even if you just slightly move your head. I'm getting this while being near the transmitter and within line of sight. I tried the various settings on the transmitter and the auto-connect on the headset, none of them made a difference. I don't know if Wifi or smartphone or other technology is causing interference, but I'm not going to shutdown everything every time I want to use the RF headphones.

At the same spots, the generic As Seen On TV headsets you'd find in stores did not have an issue maintaining a connection. They had all kinds of other issues, but the sound is kinda important in a headset.

I do not recommend them at the current $100+ price. If they go on clearance for 1/3rd or less, assuming you can ignore or survive the clicking / reconnecting sounds, everything else about them is good. They have solid construction, the headset can use standard AAA batteries if the included battery pack runs out while you are in the middle of a movie or a TV show, there is a tactile volume wheel on the headset, meaningful lights on the base and the headset.

The base (the transmitter part that connects to your audio source) has to be connected to AC power all the time. There is no battery option for the base. The headset comes with a removable battery pack that charges when you rest the headset on the base. Or you can use your own two AAA batteries.

Charging takes a while (an overnight task or day-long when you are away from them). The base has a red light on while charging and it turns off after charging is complete. Battery life is very good, I never had them run out during a single day. I don't recharge them every night, I wait until I have a ballpark of 6-8 hours or so (I don't want them to run out in the middle of the next time I use).

Input is a standard 3.5mm audio port (1/8"). If you want to connect an RCA source, you have to use your own adapter (they are cheap and easy to find but it would have been nice if Sony included one to make it easier for new buyers). A 3.5mm audio cable is included or you can use your own if you prefer a longer or shorter one.

The headset has a power on/off button. The base does not. It activates when sound is playing and it has a blinking green light. Once the headset is turned on and connected, it is a solid green light. The lights are not annoyingly bright.

title: Disappointing for the price
text:
If this was a $20 headphone, its sin could be forgiven, but with a price of over $100, one cannot excuse a wireless headphone that keeps on clicking and making reconnecting sounds even if you just slightly move your head. I'm getting this while being near the transmitter and within line of sight. I tried the various settings on the transmitter and the auto-connect on the headset, none of them made a difference. I don't know if Wifi or smartphone or other technology is causing interference, but I'm not going to shutdown everything every time I want to use the RF headphones.

At the same spots, the generic As Seen On TV headsets you'd find in stores did not have an issue maintaining a connection. They had all kinds of other issues, but the sound is kinda important in a headset.

I do not recommend them at the current $100+ price. If they go on clearance for 1/3rd or less, assuming you can ignore or survive the clicking / reconnecting sounds, everything else about them is good. They have solid construction, the headset can use standard AAA batteries if the included battery pack runs out while you are in the middle of a movie or a TV show, there is a tactile volume wheel on the headset, meaningful lights on the base and the headset.

The base (the transmitter part that connects to your audio source) has to be connected to AC power all the time. There is no battery option for the base. The headset comes with a removable battery pack that charges when you rest the headset on the base. Or you can use your own two AAA batteries.

Charging takes a while (an overnight task or day-long when you are away from them). The base has a red light on while charging and it turns off after charging is complete. Battery life is very good, I never had them run out during a single day. I don't recharge them every night, I wait until I have a ballpark of 6-8 hours or so (I don't want them to run out in the middle of the next time I use).

Input is a standard 3.5mm audio port (1/8"). If you want to connect an RCA source, you have to use your own adapter (they are cheap and easy to find but it would have been nice if Sony included one to make it easier for new buyers). A 3.5mm audio cable is included or you can use your own if you prefer a longer or shorter one.

The headset has a power on/off button. The base does not. It activates when sound is playing and it has a blinking green light. Once the headset is turned on and connected, it is a solid green light. The lights are not annoyingly bright.

The base (the transmitter part that connects to your audio source) has to be connected to AC power all the time. There is no battery option for the base. The headset comes with a removable battery pack that charges when you rest the headset on the base. Or you can use your own two AAA batteries.

Charging takes a while (an overnight task or day-long when you are away from them). The base has a red light on while charging and it turns off after charging is complete. Battery life is very good, I never had them run out during a single day. I don't recharge them every night, I wait until I have a ballpark of 6-8 hours or so (I don't want them to run out in the middle of the next time I use).

Input is a standard 3.5mm audio port (1/8"). If you want to connect an RCA source, you have to use your own adapter (they are cheap and easy to find but it would have been nice if Sony included one to make it easier for new buyers). A 3.5mm audio cable is included or you can use your own if you prefer a longer or shorter one.

The headset has a power on/off button. The base does not. It activates when sound is playing and it has a blinking green light. Once the headset is turned on and connected, it is a solid green light. The lights are not annoyingly bright.
review_full:
Disappointing for the price If this was a $20 headphone, its sin could be forgiven, but with a price of over $100, one cannot excuse a wireless headphone that keeps on clicking and making reconnecting sounds even if you just slightly move your head. I'm getting this while being near the transmitter and within line of sight. I tried the various settings on the transmitter and the auto-connect on the headset, none of them made a difference. I don't know if Wifi or smartphone or other technology is causing interference, but I'm not going to shutdown everything every time I want to use the RF headphones.

At the same spots, the generic As Seen On TV headsets you'd find in stores did not have an issue maintaining a connection. They had all kinds of other issues, but the sound is kinda important in a headset.

I do not recommend them at the current $100+ price. title: Very Poor customer service defective product
text:
I get electronics can be defective how you resolve those defects is an entirely different thing. I called Amazon support to advise the Bose would not pair with my iPhone or iPad . I went through all the trouble shooting of their (Amazon) suggestions and nothing worked. A Amazon agent Shekiera F called Bose and she called me back on the phone and told me Bose would send a new head set out immediately at no cost and there was no need to return the defective set. This was around January 12, 2020. I called Amazon on 1/23/20 to ask the status of the Use head set and was told Shekiera F did not make notes from her call with Bose even though she sent me an email from the initial call to follow up with (EMAIL sent
Amazon below (she of course didn't include the email after her return call with an update on what Bose told her about replacing my defective head setat no cost or return ) . I called Bose today nd was told by Mike (he claimed to have no employee Identifying number or any other information to identify him beyond Mike if Mike was really his name. He totally kicked the can back down the road and told me to contact Amazon. I had major throat surgery two days ago and conversations are very hard and music is helping me to escape while in silence however the defective one head set doesnt work so silence is my option. Amazon is sending a replacement out however I am unable to drive to return the defective part for two weeks and apparently no one at Amazon or Bose are concerned about these two charges sitting on my account until I can get a cleared doctors order to now have to dru=ive to a UPS store to return a product I was told didn't have to be returned. Customer service is the worst experience I have had with Amazon and Bose and I mean the worst with any of my dealings with customer service period. Dont recommend the Bose because if you get a bad pair you go through a game of kick the can down the road

title: Great Sound Very poor design for comfort and fit
text: I love being able to listen to my movies and shows without the wife barging in constantly asking me to turn the tv down , ptsd from when I was growing up and parents constantly asking me to turn things down......I gave this a 2 because the comfort of the headphones are lacking and the pleather material is also very hot and uncomfortable. They offer a cloth type material on another model but I didn’t want to spend an additional $100 + for additional comfort that should be included In the $248 I spent on these. The design needs to go back for some serious R&D!!

title: Volume all the way up and can barely hear my music.
text: I had High hopes for these but they just never panned out.  No matter how high the volume or how much I tried to custom fit the buds I could never get good volume and in fact my left ear specially could hardly hear anything.  I just couldn't get them to work for me so I purchased iPod Pro and what a difference.  Not only can I hear everything crisp and clear they have great noise canceling tech also. Sorry I wasted money on these Bose.

title: 30 Days & Dead
text:
Died almost right around 30 days. Used for running 2-3x a week for 1-2 hours at a time, used other times for just listening to music around the house. My guess is sweat corroded the connections - it just outright stopped working. Returned to AMZ.

It had its good points. Very cheap, stays put/fit, Bluetooth range, 4.1, ear tips, carrying case, long battery life. It lasted 6-8 hours easily per charge. Bluetooth reception was very good compared to others I've had. The rear wire/connecting cable is stiff and made to exert force to keep them in place - but it was comfy for hours.

Sound quality on this was terrible. Absolutely no bass response. But, at this price range it's what you'd expect. Also, the on/off sound was extremely loud.

Ended up buying another product which incidentally was from Soundpeats (same company). Keeping my fingers crossed.

title: test it while running/doing your activity first!
text:
I was really excited when I first found this product as I really like Anker products, and it had such great reviews. The first time I purchased this item, I tried to see if it connected and all that. It connected and seemed to be good, however I did not take it out for any activities until about a month later, after the return period. At that point, I found out that yes the headphones work while sitting still, but once I was doing my outside work, just about every time I would bend over or turn my head or do much of anything, the headphones would get static-y and cause my music to speed up as if the connection was bad and it needed to catch up. At this point it was past the return period, and when I attempted to contact Anker via the seller profile, I never got any kind of response.
I ended to trying to purchase a second pair, hoping that these would work and that the first pair were defective. The second pair did the same thing! I tried taking my phone out of its case, resetting my phone, bluetooth on/off many times, and I can't seem to find any troubleshooting help online. I am returning the second pair, and I hopefully can find a decent pair elsewhere. I still like Anker products, but I'm incredibly disappointed with my experience with this product.
Edit: Still disappointed with this speific product, but Anker customer service did reply to my emails and offered a different model replacement headphones that I am happy with. I am still happy with the brand, but not this product.

title: Been putting this review off for awhile.
text:
Usually my reviews are very short and sweet but this is not one of those reviews sadly this will be my first actual negative review on an Amazon product.
So let me start of by saying for a solid 7 years now since music devices first started blowing up I've had earphones in my ears constantly playing the music I love. For those 7 years I've only used corded earphones, mainly skullcandys or gummies. This past year I saw everyone wearing these Bluetooth earphones so I told myself why not try it! I had grown tired of the cords of my earphones unplugging while I ran or catching on doorknobs or chairs as I got up or walked past them. So I proceeded to do my research online and asking people who had them all my research had came back positive so I proceeded with the purchase. I've had these Bluetooth earphones for about 6 months now and I absolutely hate them with a passion. I'll start of with the few positive aspects of this Bluetooth device. It is quite well made and durable I've given them a beating with the constantly working out sweating and running I do and still they continue working. They hardly ever fallout compared to my cord earphones so that a huge positive and the button interface is simple and easy to use.
That's all I have to say for positives.
Onto the negative side.
When I first purchased these the Bluetooth range was outstanding I was impressed now when my phones in my pockets and it moves around funny the music jumps or cracks making my ear drums bleed! I put my phone in a gym sleeve or athletic band whatever you want to call so I could run and the earphones basically cut out or crack again rupturing my eardrums rendering me unable to use the gym sleeve therefore making me hold my phone in my hand as I run which is incredibly infuriating. While we're on the subject of running these things flop all over the place on my neck like a chicken with its head cut off!!! Constantly hitting my collar bones and becoming unbalanced on either side so I'm constantly stuffing them back in my shirt or fixing them. This completely takes away my concentration from breathing correctly and my runners high. Then when I'm freaking trying to lift say for example doing flat bench situps or any type of exercise on my back these dam things fallout also yanking the earphones out embarrassing me because I forgot to stuff them into my shirt! Now the sound and clarity isn't to bad nor the bass but the volume levels are basically either super quiet to where it doesn't isolate anything or loud to wear you'll probably lose your hearing after continued use. I curse these dam things! Once these things break if they ever do I'll be going back to corded earphones or at the very least a non collar looking Bluetooth headset.

title: Been putting this review off for awhile.
text:
Usually my reviews are very short and sweet but this is not one of those reviews sadly this will be my first actual negative review on an Amazon product.
So let me start of by saying for a solid 7 years now since music devices first started blowing up I've had earphones in my ears constantly playing the music I love. For those 7 years I've only used corded earphones, mainly skullcandys or gummies. This past year I saw everyone wearing these Bluetooth earphones so I told myself why not try it! I had grown tired of the cords of my earphones unplugging while I ran or catching on doorknobs or chairs as I got up or walked past them. So I proceeded to do my research online and asking people who had them all my research had came back positive so I proceeded with the purchase. I've had these Bluetooth earphones for about 6 months now and I absolutely hate them with a passion. I'll start of with the few positive aspects of this Bluetooth device. It is quite well made and durable I've given them a beating with the constantly working out sweating and running I do and still they continue working. They hardly ever fallout compared to my cord earphones so that a huge positive and the button interface is simple and easy to use.
That's all I have to say for positives.
Onto the negative side.
When I first purchased these the Bluetooth range was outstanding I was impressed now when my phones in my pockets and it moves around funny the music jumps or cracks making my ear drums bleed! I put my phone in a gym sleeve or athletic band whatever you want to call so I could run and the earphones basically cut out or crack again rupturing my eardrums rendering me unable to use the gym sleeve therefore making me hold my phone in my hand as I run which is incredibly infuriating. While we're on the subject of running these things flop all over the place on my neck like a chicken with its head cut off!!! Constantly hitting my collar bones and becoming unbalanced on either side so I'm constantly stuffing them back in my shirt or fixing them. This completely takes away my concentration from breathing correctly and my runners high. Then when I'm freaking trying to lift say for example doing flat bench situps or any type of exercise on my back these dam things fallout also yanking the earphones out embarrassing me because I forgot to stuff them into my shirt! Now the sound and clarity isn't to bad nor the bass but the volume levels are basically either super quiet to where it doesn't isolate anything or loud to wear you'll probably lose your hearing after continued use. I curse these dam things! Once these things break if they ever do I'll be going back to corded earphones or at the very least a non collar looking Bluetooth headset.

So I proceeded to do my research online and asking people who had them all my research had came back positive so I proceeded with the purchase. I've had these Bluetooth earphones for about 6 months now and I absolutely hate them with a passion. I'll start of with the few positive aspects of this Bluetooth device. It is quite well made and durable I've given them a beating with the constantly working out sweating and running I do and still they continue working. They hardly ever fallout compared to my cord earphones so that a huge positive and the button interface is simple and easy to use.
That's all I have to say for positives.
Onto the negative side.
When I first purchased these the Bluetooth range was outstanding I was impressed now when my phones in my pockets and it moves around funny the music jumps or cracks making my ear drums bleed! I put my phone in a gym sleeve or athletic band whatever you want to call so I could run and the earphones basically cut out or crack again rupturing my eardrums rendering me unable to use the gym sleeve therefore making me hold my phone in my hand as I run which is incredibly infuriating. While we're on the subject of running these things flop all over the place on my neck like a chicken with its head cut off!!! Constantly hitting my collar bones and becoming unbalanced on either side so I'm constantly stuffing them back in my shirt or fixing them. This completely takes away my concentration from breathing correctly and my runners high. Then when I'm freaking trying to lift say for example doing flat bench situps or any type of exercise on my back these dam things fallout also yanking the earphones out embarrassing me because I forgot to stuff them into my shirt! Now the sound and clarity isn't to bad nor the bass but the volume levels are basically either super quiet to where it doesn't isolate anything or loud to wear you'll probably lose your hearing after continued use. I curse these dam things! Once these things break if they ever do I'll be going back to corded earphones or at the very least a non collar looking Bluetooth headset.

title: Nice sound and fit, but I got unlucky
text:
Maybe I was unlucky, but had several issues with this product: (1) headphone didn’t turn off, so battery life was pretty low; (2) volume button got stuck frequently and it would mute the headphone.

I’ve tried force resetting a couple of times, didn’t solve the issue. Besides that, the product is amazing. I just think that when you get a $100 headset, it should be free of defects and I should not “fight” to make the product work, so I returned them

title: Great customer service but the product falls short
text:
UPDATE:
6 months after the company replaced my original pair, they were still working, but now a year later, the new pair has also died. The headphones won't hold a charge at all. It's unfortunate because I liked them but I can't replace them twice per year. Looking for another headphone company now.

EDIT: Soundpeats replaced my headphones 6 months ago (which was the amount of time it took the original pair to die) and they are still going strong! I have recommended this brand to countless people looking for an affordable quality product. These stay in the ear no matter how sweaty or how much jumping around/running I do, they hold their charge for a good amount of time and they are easy to connect to your phone. I would buy these again and again!

Originally I posted a review complaining the battery life began to suffer after 6 months of daily use. Soundpeats contacted me directly and replaced my headphones free of charge. I hope not to run into the battery problem again but I am very impressed with how quickly they reached out to correct the problem!

title: Great customer service but the product falls short
text:
UPDATE:
6 months after the company replaced my original pair, they were still working, but now a year later, the new pair has also died. The headphones won't hold a charge at all. It's unfortunate because I liked them but I can't replace them twice per year. Looking for another headphone company now.

EDIT: Soundpeats replaced my headphones 6 months ago (which was the amount of time it took the original pair to die) and they are still going strong! I have recommended this brand to countless people looking for an affordable quality product. These stay in the ear no matter how sweaty or how much jumping around/running I do, they hold their charge for a good amount of time and they are easy to connect to your phone. I would buy these again and again!

Originally I posted a review complaining the battery life began to suffer after 6 months of daily use. Soundpeats contacted me directly and replaced my headphones free of charge. I hope not to run into the battery problem again but I am very impressed with how quickly they reached out to correct the problem!

title: Not Worth the Price!
text: I purchased this item the second week in July and by the end of October the left ear bud began to crackle and I had difficulty hearing the caller as well as the caller hearing me. If this was just a $20 headset that would be one thing but the fact that I paid over $100 and it stops working only after 3 months makes me sick to my stomach. I've tried contacting the seller several times to exchange the headset but they have neglected to respond. I really enjoyed this headset at first but now I am completely dissatisfied. My suggestion is to look for a better quality headset before spending this kind of $$$!

title: Return!
text:
I returned for the following reasons
1) does not block wind. The 3 pairs of cheap $30 headphones I've had for 6 years blocked wind. I'm not sure if it's because the speakers stick out far, or the rubber ear does not insert as tightly, but there were parts of my walk outside that I couldn't hear a podcast because of the wind, which wasn't even that strong.
2) No magnetic attachment. When I have my headphones not on I like them to be magnetically clasped around my neck. The alligator clip and weight of the headphones helped some, but not enough
3) Battery life is too low. Previous headphones I've worn had a 9 hour battery life, which meant very sporadic charging. After a few days I felt the need to charge these every day.

I did like
1) sound quality
2) quick connection via bluetooth
3) easy turn on off with an easy press
4) I can put my phone down and walk away and it wouldn't drop.

review_full:
Return! I returned for the following reasons
1) does not block wind. The 3 pairs of cheap $30 headphones I've had for 6 years blocked wind. I'm not sure if it's because the speakers stick out far, or the rubber ear does not insert as tightly, but there were parts of my walk outside that I couldn't hear a podcast because of the wind, which wasn't even that strong.
2) No magnetic attachment. When I have my headphones not on I like them to be magnetically clasped around my neck. The alligator clip and weight of the headphones helped some, but not enough
3) Battery life is too low. Previous headphones I've worn had a 9 hour battery life, which meant very sporadic charging. After a few days I felt the need to charge these every day.

I did like
1) sound quality
2) quick connection via bluetooth
3) easy turn on off with an easy press
4) I can put my phone down and walk away and it wouldn't drop.

title: Very poor quality
text: I had two of these units fail on me and have other quality problems.  In the first one, one of the speakers stopped working.  It was within the one year warranty period and I got a replacement.  The replacement caused more problems.  It would occasionally get into a mode where all speech (incoming and outgoing) was garbled.  Turning the unit off and back on fixed the problem.  The microphone picked up and amplified background noise so much that I had to stay completely still if I was talking to someone through the headset.  If I did anything that made any noise, the person on the other end could not speak to me because of the loud background noises.  Finally, and again, one of the ear pieces stopped working again.  I was going to contact LG warranty again, but the serial number on the unit, which was a sticker, seems to have fallen off.  What a waste of money...

title: Garbage headphones, cracked in 10 uses
text:
Garbage headphones, cracked in 10 uses. Listened to music 10 times, didn't overextend the band or drop it, and it cracked along the band.
Called Sony customer support "nothing they can do". Amazon customer service was helpful in getting me in touch, but the Sony folks said defective products aren't covered under warranty. $200 headphones shouldn't have plastic so cheap it cracks after a week. Don't waste your money.

title: Poor sound quality. Loose fit.
text:
I was just so disappointed as I had gotten these for the Bose's renowned sound quality. Turned out the sound quality was so very poor. They didn't quite fit in my ears either and almost fell off while working out.

I returned these and have purchased a Sony pair at a cheaper price and the sound quality is so much better.

I returned these and have purchased a Sony pair at a cheaper price and the sound quality is so much better.

title: Great concept but lacks functionality
text: I love bose and own three different pairs of wired and a noise canceling set.  This is the first pair that I was truly disappointed with.  I workout and run a lot, and needed a good fitness pair of headphones.  The sound wasn't as good as bose is known for and the fit was terrible.  I tried all different size ear pieces and none fit well enough to keep the right ear piece in place due to the LARGE control box.  It's ridiculously heavy for a pair of headphones and absurdly large.

title: Uncomfortable, EQ needs setting per device, NC unimpressive, no EQ on Windows PC
text:
These headphones are heavy, tight on my small head, hot (immediately), and just uncomfortable from the first moment. Then, they easily paired to my PC but I could hardly understand any voice media. All low end sounded clean and clear but there was no high end at all. So, I read up on how to equalize them on a PC. The preferred method is to download and app. Huh? Finally, the noise cancellation goes from HMMMMMM to hmmmmmm. But the hum remains. I was unimpressed with the NC.

Possibly, if you find them comfortable for you and you are using them paired with a phone, then they may work as there is a connect app from Sony to do EQ. But these earphones are going back. Very bad experience for PC use.

title: Uncomfortable, EQ needs setting per device, NC unimpressive, no EQ on Windows PC
text:
These headphones are heavy, tight on my small head, hot (immediately), and just uncomfortable from the first moment. Then, they easily paired to my PC but I could hardly understand any voice media. All low end sounded clean and clear but there was no high end at all. So, I read up on how to equalize them on a PC. The preferred method is to download and app. Huh? Finally, the noise cancellation goes from HMMMMMM to hmmmmmm. But the hum remains. I was unimpressed with the NC.

Possibly, if you find them comfortable for you and you are using them paired with a phone, then they may work as there is a connect app from Sony to do EQ. But these earphones are going back. Very bad experience for PC use.

Possibly, if you find them comfortable for you and you are using them paired with a phone, then they may work as there is a connect app from Sony to do EQ. But these earphones are going back. Very bad experience for PC use.

title: Blah, disappointing
text:
I bought these to try out as a replacement for the Galaxy Buds Pro.
I tried these one and they fit fine.
They are comfy with no issue of staying in during workouts or walking, running, etc.
The app itself is loaded with features that are mostly useless and do not work.
The Google or Alexa AI would be great, if it worked.
The 360 audio wants to to subscribe to a service for it but does not work when playing a native 360 audio mp3 file. Also, the sound is sub par and mono.
There is no rich bass or bass sound. The bass is mid range and not deep.
I lacks the punch in sound that it should have for that price point.
Also, the noise canceling is fair, but not as good as other earbuds and not worth it.
Even with it on, I can still have a conversation, even though ambient sounds were off.
I would not purchase any of these ear buds.
review_full:
Blah, disappointing I bought these to try out as a replacement for the Galaxy Buds Pro.
I tried these one and they fit fine.
They are comfy with no issue of staying in during workouts or walking, running, etc.
The app itself is loaded with features that are mostly useless and do not work.
The Google or Alexa AI would be great, if it worked.
The 360 audio wants to to subscribe to a service for it but does not work when playing a native 360 audio mp3 file. Also, the sound is sub par and mono.
There is no rich bass or bass sound. The bass is mid range and not deep.
I lacks the punch in sound that it should have for that price point.
Also, the noise canceling is fair, but not as good as other earbuds and not worth it.
Even with it on, I can still have a conversation, even though ambient sounds were off.
I would not purchase any of these ear buds.

product_title: Sony WF-1000XM4 Industry Leading Noise Canceling Truly Wireless Earbud Headphones with Alexa Built-in, Black
features: ['Industry-leading noise canceling bluetooth earbuds with the new Integrated Processor V1.', 'Exceptional sound quality with new Integrated Processor V1 and supporting LDAC codec.', 'Crystal-clear call quality, beamforming microphone and a bone-conduction sensor provide clear voice detection even in noisy environments', 'Speak-to-chat technology automatically reduces volume during conversations.Note:If you face issue in Bluetooth connectivity please turn off the Bluetooth function for a couple of minutes, then turn it back on', '8 hours long battery with Noise Canceling', 'IPX4 Water resistance', 'Multipoint connection lets you connect two devices at once (Available via firmware update)', 'Easy wireless charging with Qi technology', '"Sony | Headphones Connect” App for Android /iO', 'These noise cancelling headphones are Alexa enabled for voice access to music, information, and more. Activate with a simple touch.']
description: ['Take the next step in truly wireless noise canceling performance and exceptional sound quality. title: Blah, disappointing
text:
I bought these to try out as a replacement for the Galaxy Buds Pro.
I tried these one and they fit fine.
They are comfy with no issue of staying in during workouts or walking, running, etc.
The app itself is loaded with features that are mostly useless and do not work.
The Google or Alexa AI would be great, if it worked.
The 360 audio wants to to subscribe to a service for it but does not work when playing a native 360 audio mp3 file. Also, the sound is sub par and mono.
There is no rich bass or bass sound. The bass is mid range and not deep.
I lacks the punch in sound that it should have for that price point.
Also, the noise canceling is fair, but not as good as other earbuds and not worth it.
Even with it on, I can still have a conversation, even though ambient sounds were off.
I would not purchase any of these ear buds.

title: Horrible range
text: If I put my phone in my pants pocket and have my hand in the pocket as well, putting a barrier(my hand), and two feet of space between the headset and my phone, its too much! Maybe, if I refrain from putting my hand in my pocket, I might be able to get through a song without it acting like a discman that's being run with, vigorously. I've had good experiences with all my other skullcandy products but as my first break into Bluetooth, it's not great.
